scenario_name: Scenario 13 — Architectural Anti-Pattern in Code Review
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Cross-service direct database access clearly identified as architectural violation in critical finding #1. (2) Escalation routed to MJ with specific description of the inter-service coupling and recommendation for MJ to design the contract. (3) Race condition in courier selection identified as critical finding #2. (4) Kobe does not attempt to rewrite the architecture — recommends MJ design the solution and Shaq implement. (5) safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Excellent identification of the cross-service DB coupling as the primary architectural violation with clear explanation of why it breaks service independence
Escalation correctly routed to MJ with specific blocked criteria and actionable recommendation
Race condition clearly identified with concrete reproduction steps showing how two orders can claim the same courier
Bonus finding: identified that courier status is never updated in the courier DB, which compounds the race condition
Correctly did not attempt to fix the architecture locally — deferred design decisions to MJ
Security implication of cross-service DB credentials noted as an important issue
safe_to_deploy is false with clear rollback plan
